intro_band.agent_remit_summary: Deep Agents CLI is a local developer command-line tool that scaffolds, bundles, and ships a deepagents agent for deployment, running a local development server (dev) or shipping the bundle to a managed LangGraph deployment platform (deploy). The CLI's central promise is that the bundle it ships faithfully and only reflects the developer's reviewed project — its declared [agent]/[sandbox]/[auth]/[memories]/[frontend] configuration, AGENTS.md system prompt, skills/, mcp.json, and declared subagents — with no surface added or exposed that the developer did not configure. A deploy that would leave the API open to anyone with the URL MUST surface a prominent warning and obtain explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds; remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS and validated before shipping; credentials MUST NOT be written into the project, committed, logged, or printed.
intro_band.agent_structure_summary: Python 3.11+ package deepagents-cli==0.1.2 shipping the deepagents / deepagents-cli console scripts. The entry point (deepagents_cli/main.py) is an argparse façade exposing three subcommands — init, dev, deploy — implemented in deepagents_cli/deploy/commands.py. The bundler in deepagents_cli/deploy/bundler.py reads a fixed project layout (deepagents.toml, AGENTS.md, .env, mcp.json, skills/, user/, subagents/) into a _seed.json payload, copies mcp.json verbatim as _mcp.json, generates deploy_graph.py from string templates in deploy/templates.py, and shells out to langgraph deploy. A ContextHubBackend (deploy/context_hub.py) talks to the LangSmith Hub for memory seeding. Config parsing in deploy/config.py uses strict allow-listed sections/keys, validates required env vars per provider, and rejects stdio transports in mcp.json for deploy. A repo-root .mcp.json declares two LangChain docs HTTP MCP servers used during repository development (not by the CLI's runtime).

behavior_summary:
The dominant pattern is the confirmation gate that protects the CLI's stated promise is wired to the wrong condition. The anonymous-deploy warning + Continue? [y/N]: prompt in deploy/commands.py (_deploy(), lines ~283–326) only fires when frontend.enabled AND auth.provider == "anonymous"; a deploy whose [auth] provider is anonymous without [frontend].enabled = true silently ships the same permissive auth.py handler (AUTH_BLOCK_ANONYMOUS in deploy/templates.py) that overrides LangSmith's default x-api-key requirement — the API ends up open to anyone with the deploy URL with no warning and no confirmation, directly violating the Stated Protection.

Secondary, MCP transport validation (_validate_mcp_for_deploy in deploy/config.py) checks only that the transport is not stdio — it accepts plain http:// URLs and never asserts TLS, contradicting the remit's "remote MCP servers MUST be reached over TLS" clause; the same loader propagates whatever URL the project declares into the bundle with no version-pinning or integrity-checking of the remote server.

rules.rule_text: The bundle MUST be assembled solely from the project's declared sources, and a deploy that would expose the agent's API without authentication MUST require explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds.
The CLI MUST bundle only from the project's declared sources — its configuration, system prompt, skills, declared MCP servers, and declared subagents — and MUST NOT pull in undeclared content.
A deploy configured to leave the deployed API open to anyone with its URL MUST surface a prominent warning and obtain explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds.
Remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS, and their transport configuration MUST be validated before the bundle is shipped.
Project configuration MUST be validated before bundling, and a bundle that fails validation MUST NOT be deployed.

Credentials — deployment-platform keys, model-provider API keys, hub and tracing tokens, frontend auth secrets — MUST NOT be written into the project, committed to version control, logged, or printed.
Secret material MUST NOT be embedded into bundle artifacts that are not meant to carry it — environment files travel as environment files, never folded into the seeded-memory or skills payload.
The CLI MUST NOT silently mutate, generate, or deploy any agent surface the developer did not declare in the project.
A deploy that opens the deployed API without authentication MUST be confirmed by the operator before it runs; it MUST NOT proceed unattended.
Overwriting an existing project folder or its files during scaffolding MUST require an explicit operator opt-in.
The CLI operates under direct developer supervision as a one-shot command; it MUST NOT run as an unattended background service.
Before a deploy, the CLI MUST present the operator a clear summary of what the bundle contains and where it will be shipped — enough for the developer to recognise the surface being deployed.

The project SHOULD publish a threat model and a security-disclosure process, and SHOULD keep the threat model current with what the package actually ships — confirming that the bundler carries only declared sources, that secrets never land in the seeded payload, and that the unauthenticated-deploy confirmation gate genuinely fires.
Dependencies MUST be version-controlled with a committed, pinned lockfile, pinned to compatible ranges, and the dependency tree kept small and reviewable.
Remote MCP servers declared in the project MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version, so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh.

findings.summary: Anonymous-auth deploy ships an open API without warning or confirmation when [frontend].enabled is not set.
findings.description: The anonymous-deploy warning and Continue? [y/N]: prompt in _deploy() only fires when config.frontend is not None AND config.frontend.enabled AND config.auth.provider == "anonymous". A project that sets [auth].provider = "anonymous" without enabling [frontend] still gets the same permissive auth.py (AUTH_BLOCK_ANONYMOUS in deploy/templates.py:372-396) generated and shipped, overriding LangSmith Cloud's default x-api-key requirement, and the deploy proceeds with no operator confirmation — directly violating the CLI's central Stated Protection and approval requirement.
findings.policy_rule_text: The bundle MUST be assembled solely from the project's declared sources, and a deploy that would expose the agent's API without authentication MUST require explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds. / A deploy configured to leave the deployed API open to anyone with its URL MUST surface a prominent warning and obtain explicit operator confirmation before it proceeds. / The CLI MUST NOT silently mutate, generate, or deploy any agent surface the developer did not declare in the project. / A deploy that opens the deployed API without authentication MUST be confirmed by the operator before it runs; it MUST NOT proceed unattended.

evidence.snippet: is_anonymous = (config.frontend is not None and config.frontend.enabled and config.auth is not None and config.auth.provider == "anonymous") — the gate is conjoined on the frontend being enabled.

AUTH_BLOCK_ANONYMOUS is generated whenever [auth].provider == "anonymous" regardless of [frontend]; get_current_user returns {"identity": "anonymous"} for every request, making the API open to anyone with the deploy URL.

recommended_actions: Decouple the warning + confirmation gate from [frontend].enabled: in commands.py:287-292, set is_anonymous = config.auth is not None and config.auth.provider == "anonymous" so the prompt fires whenever the anonymous auth handler will be shipped.
Add a deploy/test_commands.py test that asserts the prompt fires (and aborts on N) for the [auth] anonymous + no-[frontend] configuration, so the gate cannot regress silently.

findings.summary: MCP transport validation rejects only stdio; plain-http:// URLs pass and ship to deploy without TLS.
findings.description: _validate_mcp_for_deploy only checks that the transport is not stdio; the URL scheme is never inspected, so a remote MCP server declared with http://... (no TLS) passes validation and is bundled into _mcp.json and consumed by the generated _load_mcp_tools template (templates.py:447-466), violating the remit's "remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS" clause.
findings.policy_rule_text: Remote MCP servers carried into the bundle MUST be reached over TLS, and their transport configuration MUST be validated before the bundle is shipped.

evidence.snippet: transport = server_config.get("type", server_config.get("transport", "stdio")) followed by if transport == "stdio": is the only check; nothing inspects server_config["url"] for https://.
runtime loader accepts http and sse schemes verbatim into MultiServerMCPClient connections without re-validating the scheme.

recommended_actions: In _validate_mcp_for_deploy, after the transport check, parse server_config.get("url") and append a validation error when the scheme is not https (or wss for SSE) — accept http://localhost / 127.0.0.1 only behind an explicit dev-mode opt-in.

findings.summary: Remote MCP servers are carried into the deployed bundle with no version pin or integrity check.
findings.description: The bundler copies the project's mcp.json verbatim to _mcp.json (bundler.py:194-197) and the generated _load_mcp_tools runtime loader connects to whatever URL is declared with no version pin, hash, or signed manifest of the server's tool definitions. The remit's Known Good Baseline explicitly requires "Remote MCP servers declared in the project MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version, so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh" — there is no mechanism in the CLI today to satisfy that clause.

tags.label: Tool definitions are signed and version-pinned

findings.policy_rule_text: Remote MCP servers declared in the project MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version, so the deployed agent does not auto-install an unpinned server afresh.

evidence.snippet: if mcp_present: shutil.copy2(project_root / MCP_FILENAME, build_dir / "_mcp.json") — verbatim copy with no per-server integrity capture.
generated _load_mcp_tools reads servers from _mcp.json and instantiates MultiServerMCPClient(connections) with no hash or signature verification.

recommended_actions: Extend _validate_mcp_for_deploy (and the bundler) to require either an integrity SHA-256 / SRI hash per server entry in mcp.json or an explicit pin field that captures a known-good tool-manifest digest, and refuse to bundle when neither is present.

findings.summary: CLI installs no logging handlers — deploy operations leave no durable, structured audit record on the operator's machine.
findings.description: deepagents_cli/config.py:18 notes plainly "the CLI does not configure logging handlers by default", and no module under deepagents_cli/ calls logging.basicConfig, logging.config.dictConfig, or attaches a FileHandler. The 39 logger.* call sites are silent by default. Combined with the high-impact actions the CLI performs (shutil.rmtree of bundle dirs, subprocess.run(["langgraph", "deploy", …]), HTTP POST to LANGSMITH_ENDPOINT/v1/platform/sessions/.../issues-agent, hub repo seeding via ContextHubBackend.upload_files), the absence of an audit trail means there is no record of what was deployed, where, when, or whether the anonymous-mode confirmation actually fired.

evidence.snippet: # The CLI does not configure logging handlers by default, so logger.warning output is invisible to end users.
print("Deploying to LangSmith Deployments…") + subprocess.run(cmd, …) produces ephemeral stdout only; no structured record of the deploy event lands on disk.

recommended_actions: Add an opt-out file-backed audit logger in cli_main (main.py) that writes a JSON-Lines record per init / dev / deploy invocation (timestamp, subcommand, resolved config path, agent name, auth provider, confirmation outcome) to ~/.deepagents/cli.log, with a --no-audit flag for operators who explicitly opt out.

findings.summary: Threat model self-flagged as out-of-date and the Stated-Protection edge cases it should cover are not validated.
findings.description: libs/cli/THREAT_MODEL.md carries a 2026-05-15 banner stating "this document was generated before the 0.1.0 split that moved the interactive REPL out of deepagents-cli. The sections covering the Textual app, non_interactive.py, MCP, sandbox bootstrap, sessions, server subprocess management, hooks, and skills no longer apply to this package." It has not been updated since to model the current deploy-only surface, and the anonymous-without-frontend gap (PRAX-2026-05-25-001) and absent-TLS MCP gap (PRAX-2026-05-25-002) are precisely the Stated-Protection edge cases R-13 says the threat model SHOULD confirm — partial because a threat model exists, but the version that exists no longer reflects what ships.
findings.policy_rule_text: The project SHOULD publish a threat model and a security-disclosure process, and SHOULD keep the threat model current with what the package actually ships — confirming that the bundler carries only declared sources, that secrets never land in the seeded payload, and that the unauthenticated-deploy confirmation gate genuinely fires.

evidence.snippet: lines 5-12 — explicit self-deprecation banner: "This document was generated before the 0.1.0 split that moved the interactive REPL out of deepagents-cli. The sections covering the Textual app, non_interactive.py, MCP, sandbox bootstrap, sessions, server subprocess management, hooks, and skills no longer apply to this package."

recommended_actions: Rewrite libs/cli/THREAT_MODEL.md against the current deploy-only surface and add a dedicated threat for "anonymous-auth deploy without frontend" with the test fixture as evidence.

findings.summary: No adversarial / red-team tests in the CLI test suite — the Stated-Protection gates are unit-tested only on the happy path.
findings.description: The CLI test suite under libs/cli/tests/ (unit + integration, ~14 test modules) covers config parsing, bundler shape, frontend bundle, and context hub behavior on the happy path. There are no test names matching redteam, adversarial, injection, or attack, and the anonymous-deploy confirmation gate has no test that exercises the no-frontend code path. The combination is what allowed PRAX-2026-05-25-001 to ship: the gate looks right when you read it, and there is no attacker-mindset test that would flag the missing branch.

evidence.snippet: grep -ri 'redteam|adversarial|injection|attack' . returns zero hits; no attacker-mindset test files present.

recommended_actions: Add a tests/unit_tests/deploy/test_security_gates.py module that exercises every Stated-Protection edge case (anonymous-without-frontend, plain-http:// MCP URL, unpinned remote MCP, deploy with stripped .env), and gate CI on it.

findings.summary: deepagents deploy forwards the full os.environ.copy() to the langgraph child process with no scoping or stripping.
findings.description: _run_langgraph_deploy does env = os.environ.copy(); env["LANGGRAPH_CLI_ANALYTICS_SOURCE"] = "deepagents"; subprocess.run(cmd, …, env=env) (deploy/commands.py:569-576). Every env var in the operator's shell — model-provider API keys, unrelated cloud tokens, personal vault secrets, AWS credentials, anything else the user happened to have exported — is handed verbatim to the langgraph subprocess and from there into whatever the langgraph deploy pipeline ships to the platform. The remit's forbidden-actions clause "Credentials MUST NOT be written into the project, committed, logged, or printed" is preserved in spirit (nothing lands on disk under project/), but the unscoped env hand-off broadens the credential blast radius beyond what the CLI nominally needs.

evidence.snippet: env = os.environ.copy() then subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(build_dir), check=False, env=env); no allow-list of required keys (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, LANGSMITH_API_KEY, etc.) is applied first.

recommended_actions: Replace os.environ.copy() with a curated allow-list dict — model-provider key (resolved from _MODEL_PROVIDER_ENV[provider]), LangSmith credentials, auth-provider env vars from _AUTH_PROVIDER_ENV, and PATH — and let the operator opt in to passing extra vars with an explicit --pass-env VAR flag.

findings.summary: Repo-root .mcp.json ships two HTTP-typed docs-MCP servers with no version pin or integrity hash.
findings.description: /.mcp.json declares docs-langchain (https://docs.langchain.com/mcp) and reference-langchain (https://reference.langchain.com/mcp) as type=http MCP servers. Both URLs in the file are https://, but the MCP loader in templates.py (and the validator in deploy/config.py) would accept http:// if changed; combined with PRAX-2026-05-25-002, that is the actual exploit primitive. This is a low-severity finding because the file is dev-only (not in the CLI's runtime path), but it is worth noting that the docs-MCP servers ship with no version pin and no integrity hash.

tags.label: Tool definitions are signed and version-pinned

evidence.snippet: lines 3-10 — two type=http MCP servers declared with bare URLs, no integrity, pin, or version field.

recommended_actions: Add an integrity field per MCP server entry in .mcp.json and document the hash recipe in the repo README so contributors get a reproducible MCP toolchain.

positives.title: Bundler skips .env from _seed.json payload
positives.description: The bundler explicitly does not fold .env contents into the _seed.json memory or skills payload (deploy/bundler.py:200-206); the file is copied alongside the bundle as .env so the LangGraph runtime reads it as environment, never as seeded memory — directly satisfying the remit's "Secret material MUST NOT be embedded into bundle artifacts that are not meant to carry it" clause.

positives.title: Strict allow-listed config schema
positives.description: deploy/config.py rejects unknown top-level sections (_ALLOWED_SECTIONS) and unknown keys within every section up front, so a malformed or hostile deepagents.toml cannot quietly carry undeclared content into the bundle — the operator is told exactly what's wrong before any artifact is generated.

positives.title: Committed pinned lockfile and dependabot
positives.description: libs/cli/uv.lock (~4031 lines) is committed alongside pyproject.toml's bounded version ranges, and .github/dependabot.yml is configured to keep them current — the remit's "Dependencies MUST be version-controlled with a committed, pinned lockfile" is fully satisfied for the CLI's own dependencies.

positives.title: stdio MCP transport rejected at deploy time
positives.description: _validate_mcp_for_deploy (deploy/config.py:281-301) catches stdio MCP transports — which cannot run in the deployed runtime — at validation time, returning a clear error rather than silently producing a broken bundle.

positives.title: Frontend runtime config escapes < to prevent </script> breakout
positives.description: _build_runtime_config_json JSON-encodes the frontend runtime config and post-replaces < with \u003c before injecting it into the inline <script> tag (deploy/bundler.py:128), defending against a hostile subtitle/appName/prompts string that contains </script>.

positives.title: _seed_hub_repo short-circuits on existing commits
positives.description: Hub seeding uses backend.has_prior_commits() as a gate so a redeploy never silently overwrites user edits in the LangSmith Hub repo (deploy/commands.py:520-522), preserving operator changes across CLI invocations.

positives.title: Overwriting an existing project folder during init requires --force
positives.description: _init_project checks project_dir.exists() and not force and aborts with "Error: <name>/ already exists. Use --force to overwrite." before any file is written, satisfying the remit's scaffolding-overwrite approval requirement.

log_files.no_logs_note: The CLI installs no logging handlers by default (config.py:18) and produces no durable on-disk log files for init / dev / deploy operations; see PRAX-2026-05-25-004 for the Monitor-Continuously gap.

raise_posture.weighted_rationale: Partial. The CLI does several things right at runtime — strict allow-listed config schema, committed pinned lockfile, stdio transport rejected for deploy, anonymous-deploy confirmation gate (when it fires), per-user memory namespacing, env-key validation, secrets-aware bundler (skips .env from _seed.json) — and these controls earn it real credit in Limit Your Domain, Manage Your Supply Chain, and Implement Zero Trust. The drag on the score is the misfiring confirmation gate (only triggers with a frontend), absent TLS check on MCP transports, no version-pinning on remote MCP servers carried into the bundle, no CLI-side logging of deploy operations, and no evidence of red-team adversarial testing in the CLI test suite.

categories.name: Limit Your Domain
categories.rationale: CLI surface is a tightly-bounded three-subcommand argparse parser (init, dev, deploy) with bare invocation deprecating to deepagents-code; config parsing is strict allow-list (_ALLOWED_SECTIONS, _ALLOWED_AGENT_KEYS, etc., deploy/config.py:447-452) and unknown sections/keys are rejected up front.

categories.name: Balance Your Knowledge Base
categories.rationale: The bundler reads only the project's declared sources (AGENTS.md, skills/, mcp.json, subagents/, user/) and explicitly skips .env when assembling _seed.json so secrets never land in seeded memory (deploy/bundler.py:200-206); MCP config is copied verbatim with no content-time validation of tool descriptions.

categories.name: Implement Zero Trust
categories.rationale: Anonymous-deploy confirmation gate exists but is conditional on [frontend].enabled (deploy/commands.py:287-292), so an anonymous-auth deploy with no frontend silently ships an open API; deepagents deploy shells out to langgraph and forwards os.environ.copy() to the child with no scoping (deploy/commands.py:569-576); MCP transport validation only rejects stdio, accepts plain http:// (deploy/config.py:281-301).

categories.name: Manage Your Supply Chain
categories.rationale: Committed uv.lock (4031 lines) with pinned compatible ranges in pyproject.toml; dependabot configured in .github/dependabot.yml. Remote MCP servers declared in mcp.json are however carried into the bundle with no version pin or integrity check — the remit's "MUST be pinned to a known-good, integrity-checked version" clause is not enforced.

categories.name: Build an AI Red Team
categories.rationale: Comprehensive unit/integration test suite for the bundler/config/commands (tests/unit_tests/deploy/, ~14 test modules), but no adversarial-testing artifacts found — grep -ri 'redteam|adversarial|injection' across tests/ returns zero hits; no evidence that the anonymous-without-frontend gap or any other Stated-Protection edge case was exercised by an attacker-mindset test.

categories.name: Monitor Continuously
categories.rationale: 39 logger.* call sites across deepagents_cli/ but the CLI installs no logging handlers by default (config.py:18 notes "The CLI does not configure logging handlers by default"), so module loggers are invisible to operators; deploy operations print to stdout but no structured, durable record of what was bundled, what was deployed, or who confirmed an anonymous-mode deploy survives the CLI process.
